system_prompt:
You are an expert VLSI Verification Engineer. Your task is to perform Fault Localization and RTL Repair. Analyze the provided Error Symptom, the FULL Raw VCD Trace, and Raw Verilog code. 
You must output a JSON object with exactly three keys:
1. "buggy_line": The exact integer line number from the Raw Verilog where the defect exists.
2. "root_cause": A 1-2 sentence explanation of why the logic failed at the failing timestamp.
3. "fixed_code": The corrected single line (or block) of Verilog code.
user_prompt:

=================================================================
TEST FAILED AT 280.0 ns
FAILING SIGNAL : result7
ERROR DETAILS  : Test 1: Mismatch at Matrix C[1][3]. Expected 43, got 147
=================================================================

$end
$version

   1: module design_5(inp_west0, inp_west4, inp_west8, inp_west12,
   2: 		      inp_north0, inp_north1, inp_north2, inp_north3,
   3: 		      clk, rst, done, result0, result1, result2, result3, result4, result5, result6, result7, result8, result9, result10, result11, result12, result13, result14, result15);

   7: 	output reg done;
   8:     output [63:0] result0, result1, result2, result3, result4, result5, result6, result7, result8, result9, result10, result11, result12, result13, result14, result15;

  16: 	wire [31:0] outp_south0, outp_south1, outp_south2, outp_south3, outp_south4, outp_south5, outp_south6, outp_south7, outp_south8, outp_south9, outp_south10, outp_south11, outp_south12, outp_south13, outp_south14, outp_south15;
  17: 	wire [31:0] outp_east0, outp_east1, outp_east2, outp_east3, outp_east4, outp_east5, outp_east6, outp_east7, outp_east8, outp_east9, outp_east10, outp_east11, outp_east12, outp_east13, outp_east14, outp_east15;
  18: 
  19: 
  20: 
  21: 
  22: 	//from north and west
  23: 	block P0 (inp_north0, inp_west0, clk, rst, outp_south0, outp_east0, result0);
  24: 	//from north
  25: 	block P1 (inp_north1, outp_east0, clk, rst, outp_south1, outp_east1, result1);
  26: 	block P2 (inp_north2, outp_east1, clk, rst, outp_south2, outp_east2, result2);
  27: 	block P3 (inp_north3, outp_east2, clk, rst, outp_south3, outp_east3, result3);
  28: 
  29: 	//from west
  30: 	block P4 (outp_south0, inp_west4, clk, rst, outp_south4, outp_east4, result4);
  31: 	block P8 (outp_south4, inp_west8, clk, rst, outp_south8, outp_east8, result8);
  32: 	block P12 (outp_south8, inp_west12, clk, rst, outp_south12, outp_east12, result12);
  33: 
  34: 	//no direct inputs
  35: 	//second row
  36: 	block P5 (outp_south1, outp_east4, clk, rst, outp_south5, outp_east5, result5);
  37: 	block P6 (outp_south2, outp_east5, clk, rst, outp_south6, outp_east6, result6);
  38: 	block P7 (outp_south3, outp_east5, clk, rst, outp_south7, outp_east7, result7);
  39: 	//third row
  40: 	block P9 (outp_south5, outp_east8, clk, rst, outp_south9, outp_east9, result9);
  41: 	block P10 (outp_south6, outp_east9, clk, rst, outp_south10, outp_east10, result10);
  42: 	block P11 (outp_south7, outp_east10, clk, rst, outp_south11, outp_east11, result11);
  43: 	//fourth row

  46: 	block P15 (outp_south11, outp_east14, clk, rst, outp_south15, outp_east15, result15);
  47: 
  48: 	always @(posedge clk or posedge rst) begin
  49: 		if(rst) begin
  50: 			done <= 0;
  51: 			count <= 0;
  52: 		end
  53: 		else begin
  54: 			if(count == 9) begin
  55: 				done <= 1;
  56: 				count <= 0;
  57: 			end
  58: 			else begin
  59: 				done <= 0;
  60: 				count <= count + 1;
  61: 			end
  62: 		end
  63: 	end
  64:

  66: endmodule
  67:

  69: module block(inp_north, inp_west, clk, rst, outp_south, outp_east, result);
  70: 	input [31:0] inp_north, inp_west;
  71: 	output reg [31:0] outp_south, outp_east;
  72: 	input clk, rst;
  73: 	output reg [63:0] result;
  74: 	wire [63:0] multi;
  75: 	always @(posedge rst or posedge clk) begin
  76: 		if(rst) begin
  77: 			result <= 0;
  78: 			outp_east <= 0;
  79: 			outp_south <= 0;
  80: 		end
  81: 		else begin
  82: 			result <= result + multi;
  83: 			outp_east <= inp_west;
  84: 			outp_south <= inp_north;
  85: 		end
  86: 	end
  87: 	assign multi = inp_north*inp_west;
  88: endmodule
